'''Brayden Wiliame''' (born 17 December 1992) is a former [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|ce|wg}}er and {{rlp|sr}} forward for [[New Zealand Warriors]] in the [[National Rugby League]] (NRL).

He has previously played for the [[Parramatta Eels]], [[Manly Warringah Sea Eagles]] and [[St George Illawarra Dragons]] in the (NRL), and the [[Catalans Dragons]] in the [[Super League]].

Wiliame is currently the coach of the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs Women]]'s NRLW squad.

==Background==
Wiliame was born in [[Gosford|Gosford, New South Wales]], Australia and is of [[Fijians|Fijian]] descent.

He played his junior football for the [[Terrigal Sharks|Woy Woy Roosters]], [[Umina Beach Bunnies|Umina Bunnies]] and [[Central Coast (New South Wales)|Central Coast]] [[Harold Matthews Cup]] and [[S. G. Ball Cup]] squads, before signing a 3-year contract with the [[Melbourne Storm]].

==Playing career==

===2010-2012===
From 2010 to 2012, Wiliame played for the [[Melbourne Storm]]'s [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] team.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://rleague.com/players/Brayden-Williame_2814/Matches/ |title=Brayden Williame – Player |website=rleague.com |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140527213800/http://rleague.com/players/Brayden-Williame_2814/Matches/ |archive-date=2014-05-27 |access-date=2018-02-21}}</ref>

In June 2010, Wiliame played for the [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales]] Under-18s team.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.westsydneyrl.leaguenet.com.au/index.php?id=73&tx_ttnews%5Bpointer%5D=19&tx_ttnews%5Btt_news%5D=180&tx_ttnews%5BbackPid%5D=41&cHash=644855414d |title=NSWRL Under 18's Team Announcement |website=Western Sydney Academy |publisher=leaguenet.com.au |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160204134212/http://www.westsydneyrl.leaguenet.com.au/index.php?id=73&tx_ttnews%5Bpointer%5D=19&tx_ttnews%5Btt_news%5D=180&tx_ttnews%5BbackPid%5D=41&cHash=644855414d |archive-date=2016-02-04 |access-date=2015-05-03}}</ref>

In October 2010, Wiliame played for the junior [[Fiji national rugby league team|Fiji]] team.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://fijilive.com/sports/football/news/2010/10/junior-bati-initiative-to-boost-2013-wc/17242.Fijilive |title=Junior Bati initiative to boost 2013 WC |last=Tokalau |first=Torika |date=2010-10-10 |website=Fijilive |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141028040850/http://fijilive.com/sports/football/news/2010/10/junior-bati-initiative-to-boost-2013-wc/17242.Fijilive |archive-date=2014-10-28 |access-date=2015-05-03}}</ref>

===2013===
In 2013, Wiliame joined the [[Parramatta Eels]]. In round 13 of the [[2013 NRL season]], Wiliame made his [[National Rugby League|NRL]] debut for the [[Parramatta Eels]] against the [[Sydney Roosters]].<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.parraeels.com.au/news-display/Round-13-NRL-Late-Mail/76575 |title=Round 13: NRL Late Mail |date=2013-06-07 |website=Parramatta Eels |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://archive.today/20130616025332/http://www.parraeels.com.au/news-display/Round-13-NRL-Late-Mail/76575 |archive-date=2013-06-16 |access-date=2015-03-20}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |url=http://rleague.com/players/Brayden-Willieme_3969/Matches/ |title=Brayden Willieme – Player |website=rleague.com |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140304134741/http://rleague.com/players/Brayden-Willieme_3969/Matches/ |archive-date=2014-03-04 |access-date=2018-02-21}}</ref>
He played six games for Parramatta in the [[2013 NRL season]] as the club finished last on the table for a second consecutive year. At the end of 2013, Wiliame was released by the [[Parramatta Eels]] and joined the [[Newcastle Knights]] for the [[2014 NRL season]].<ref>{{Cite news |last=Riccio |first=David |url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/parramatta-eels-work-to-erase-bitter-memories-of-2013s-wooden-spoon-season/news-story/53c0bcf5d4870490a3ab9c448bfae244/ |title=Parramatta Eels work to erase bitter memories of 2013's wooden spoon season |date=2013-12-08 |work=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191219134012/https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/parramatta-eels-work-to-erase-bitter-memories-of-2013s-wooden-spoon-season/news-story/53c0bcf5d4870490a3ab9c448bfae244/ |archive-date=2019-12-19}}</ref>

===2014===
On 1 October 2014, Wiliame signed a two-year contract with the [[Manly Warringah Sea Eagles]] starting in 2015.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.seaeagles.com.au/news/2014/10/01/promising_trio_added_to_sea_eagles_roster.html |title=Promising trio added to Sea Eagles roster |date=2014-10-01 |website=Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150331160801/http://www.seaeagles.com.au/news/2014/10/01/promising_trio_added_to_sea_eagles_roster.html |archive-date=2015-03-31 |access-date=2015-05-03}}</ref>

===2015===
He made his debut for the [[Manly Warringah Sea Eagles]] in round 3 of the [[2015 NRL season]] against the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]].<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.seaeagles.com.au/news/2015/03/20/late_mail_leary_out_.html |title=LATE MAIL: Leary out, Horo in |date=2015-03-20 |website=Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150402125402/http://www.seaeagles.com.au/news/2015/03/20/late_mail_leary_out_.html |archive-date=2015-04-02 |access-date=2015-05-03}}</ref>

===2016===
After playing on the wing in the opening two rounds of the [[2016 NRL season]], Wiliame was demoted to the Manly Warringah Sea Eagles' New South Wales Cup team to allow for the return from injury of fullback [[Brett Stewart (rugby league)|Brett Stewart]]. He was recalled to the side for the Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles' [[Anzac Day]] match against the [[Newcastle Knights]] in [[Newcastle, New South Wales|Newcastle]] following a broken collar bone suffered by winger [[Jorge Taufua]] a week earlier against [[Parramatta Eels|Parramatta]]. Wiliame crossed for his first NRL try of the season. [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]] ran out 26-10 winners over the bottom placed Newcastle.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.nrl.com/injuries-strike-as-manly-down-newcastle/tabid/10874/newsid/95228/default.aspx |title=Injuries strike as Manly down Newcastle |last=Brady |first=Jack |date=2016-04-25 |publisher=National Rugby League |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160426113929/http://www.nrl.com/injuries-strike-as-manly-down-newcastle/tabid/10874/newsid/95228/default.aspx |archive-date=2016-04-26}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/other-sports/list/the-lurker-revealed-you-wont-believe-who-manly-want-to-re-sign-brayden-williame/1726cz73kjpfg1d8h2d5zvds6x/|title=The Lurker: Revealed – You won't believe who Manly want to re-sign|website=sportingnews.com}}</ref>

After 23 first grade games with [[Manly Warringah Sea Eagles]], Wiliame was granted a release from his contract with the club on 9 November 2016 in order to take up a two-year deal with the [[Catalans Dragons]] in the [[Super League]].<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.seaeagles.com.au/news/2016/11/09/catalans_sign_brayde.html |title=Catalans sign Brayden Wiliame |date=2016-11-09 |website=Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20161109221208/http://www.seaeagles.com.au/news/2016/11/09/catalans_sign_brayde.html |archive-date=2016-11-09}}</ref>

===2018===
He played in the [[2018 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over the [[Warrington Wolves]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref>{{Cite news |last=Bower |first=Aaron |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2018/aug/25/catalans-dragons-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final-match-report |title=Catalans Dragons beat Warrington in Challenge Cup final to make history |date=2018-08-25 |work=The Guardian |access-date=2019-11-05 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190905200224/https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2018/aug/25/catalans-dragons-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final-match-report |archive-date=2019-09-05}}</ref>

===2019===
On 17 December 2019, Wiliame signed a two-year deal to join [[St. George Illawarra Dragons|St. George Illawarra]] starting in the [[2020 NRL season]].<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/st-george-illawarra-confirms-signing-of-centre-brayden-williame-on-two-year-deal/kkfdsv0hbcnj17jzobdp1zlmr/ |title=St. George Illawarra confirms signing of centre Brayden Williame on two-year deal |last=Chisholm |first=Ed |date=2019-12-17 |website=Sporting News |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191217175013/https://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/st-george-illawarra-confirms-signing-of-centre-brayden-williame-on-two-year-deal/kkfdsv0hbcnj17jzobdp1zlmr/ |archive-date=2019-12-17}}</ref>

===2020-2021===
On 16 September 2021, it was announced that Williame had been released by [[St. George Illawarra]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2021-st-georgeillawarra-dragons-gold-coast-titans-departing-players-corey-norman-ashley-taylor-tyrone-peachey-transfer-centre-signings/news-story/a6c1cdb196e86219a8075fa120d8ae69/|title=Bulldogs' backline boost as rising star re-signs; Eels hooker seals UK exit: Transfer Centre|publisher=Fox Sports|location=Australia|date=16 September 2021}}</ref>

===2022===
On 11 November, Wiliame signed a two-year deal to join the [[New Zealand Warriors]] starting in 2023.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://uk.sports.yahoo.com/news/former-catalans-outside-back-brayden-100013788.html?guccounter=1&guce_referrer=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuZ29vZ2xlLmNvbS8&guce_referrer_sig=AQAAAGgeV60pe-w0v4KN7kKs8F8emwPwFlbPGmARe3vBIpQrIVhw1fe4CQaTVWVh4AGoFNcMh45Y0PuYFhL2qVzWV9AXrVaQxrK9XCfxVcbZ3TfR2kEo9PTq4WWKK2DsuAwswh1S-KeamPFs9dxPN4wPn_kRyZpeH2qLCbz-oqHUL80l/|title=Former Catalans outside-back Brayden Wiliame joins NRL club|website=uk.sports.yahoo.com}}</ref>

===2023===
Wiliame was limited to only two matches for the New Zealand Warriors in the 2023 NRL season as the club finished 4th on the table and qualified for the finals.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/rugby-league/news/nrl-2023-new-zealand-warriors-season-review/8343d491a8ee8126ee2829c2#:~:text=This%20season%2C%20they%20struggled%20to,magic%20they%20had%20last%20year./|title=NRL 2023: New Zealand Warriors season review|website=sportingnews.com}}</ref>

On 11 Oct 2023 he announced his retirement from professional rugby league.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=11 October 2023|title=Former Catalans Dragons ace calls time on career: 'Rugby league, it’s been an absolute pleasure'|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/former-catalans-dragons-ace-calls-time-on-career-rugby-league-its-been-an-absolute-pleasure}}</ref>

== Coaching career ==
On 7 April 2025, the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]] announced that Wiliame had signed a two year deal and would coach their NRLW squad.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2025-04-07 |title=Wiliame Named as Bulldogs NRLW Head Coach |url=https://www.bulldogs.com.au/news/2025/04/07/wiliame-named-as-bulldogs-nrlw-head-coach/ |access-date=2025-04-07 |website=Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs |language=en}}</ref> Wiliame previously worked with the [[New Zealand Warriors]] Pathways team.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Chalk |first=Ethan Lee |date=2025-04-07 |title=Bulldogs name former NRL centre as head coach of their NRLW side |url=https://www.zerotackle.com/bulldogs-name-former-nrl-centre-as-head-coach-of-their-nrlw-side-220498/ |access-date=2025-04-07 |website=Zero Tackle |language=en-US}}</ref>

==International career==
On 2 May 2015, Wiliame played for [[Fiji national rugby league team|Fiji]] against [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]] in the [[International rugby league in 2015#Melanesian Cup|2015 Melanesian Cup]].<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.nrl.com/representative-round-team-lists/tabid/10874/newsid/85557/default.aspx |title=Representative Round team lists |date=2015-04-28 |publisher=National Rugby League |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150505202335/http://www.nrl.com/representative-round-team-lists/tabid/10874/newsid/85557/default.aspx |archive-date=2015-05-05 |access-date=2015-05-03}}</ref>

On 7 May 2016, Wiliame played for Fiji against Papua New Guinea in the [[International rugby league in 2016#Papua New Guinea vs Fiji men in Australia|2016 Melanesian Cup]].<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.nrl.com/png-stuns-fiji-in-a-thriller/tabid/10874/newsid/95678/default.aspx |title=PNG stuns Fiji in a thriller |last=Gabor |first=Martin |date=2016-05-07 |publisher=National Rugby League |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160508120344/http://www.nrl.com/png-stuns-fiji-in-a-thriller/tabid/10874/newsid/95678/default.aspx |archive-date=2016-05-08 |access-date=2016-05-07}}</ref>
